text:
[Match] 2022 FIFA Men's World Cup · Argentina vs France · final · 2022-12-18
Score: 3-3 (ET), penalties 4-2. Stadium: Lusail Stadium, Lusail.
Goals: Lionel Messi (pen) (23'), Ángel Di María (36'), Kylian Mbappé (pen) (80'), Kylian Mbappé (81'), Lionel Messi (108'), Kylian Mbappé (pen) (118').

[Match] 2018 FIFA Men's World Cup · France vs Croatia · final · 2018-07-15
Score: 4-2. Stadium: Luzhniki Stadium, Moscow.
Goals: Mario Mandžukić (OG) (18'), Ivan Perišić (28'), Antoine Griezmann (pen) (38'), Paul Pogba (59'), Kylian Mbappé (65'), Mario Mandžukić (69').

[Tournament] 2022 FIFA Men's World Cup (WC-2022)
Year: 2022. Dates: 2022-11-20 to 2022-12-18.
Host: Qatar. Winner: Argentina. Teams: 32. Includes final.

[Tournament] 2018 FIFA Men's World Cup (WC-2018)
Year: 2018. Dates: 2018-06-14 to 2018-07-15.
Host: Russia. Winner: France. Teams: 32. Includes final.

[Player Career] Lionel Messi · Men's
ID: P-14758. Teams: ARG. Born: 1987-06-24. Position: FW.
World Cups (5): 2006, 2010, 2014, 2018, 2022. Squad listings: 5.
Goals: 13 total (2006: 1, 2014: 4, 2018: 1, 2022: 7).
Appearances: 26 (24 starts, 2 substitute). Discipline: 2 yellow, 0 red.
Awards: Golden Ball (2014); Golden Ball (2022); Silver Boot (2022).

[Player Career] Kylian Mbappé · Men's
ID: P-64077. Teams: FRA. Born: 1998-12-20. Position: FW.
World Cups (2): 2018, 2022. Squad listings: 2.
Goals: 12 total (2018: 4, 2022: 8).
Appearances: 14 (12 starts, 2 substitute). Discipline: 2 yellow, 0 red.
Awards: Best Young Player (2018); Golden Boot (2022); Silver Ball (2022).

[Player Career] Diego Maradona · Men's
ID: P-80404. Teams: ARG. Born: 1960-10-30. Position: MF.
World Cups (4): 1982, 1986, 1990, 1994. Squad listings: 4.
Goals: 8 total (1982: 2, 1986: 5, 1994: 1).
Appearances: 21 (21 starts, 0 substitute). Discipline: 4 yellow, 1 red.
Awards: Golden Ball (1986); Silver Boot (1986); Bronze Ball (1990).

[Match] 2022 FIFA Men's World Cup · Qatar vs Ecuador · group stage · 2022-11-20
Score: 0-2. Stadium: Al Bayt Stadium, Al Khor.
Goals: Enner Valencia (pen) (16'), Enner Valencia (31').

Score: 2-4 (ET). Stadium: Estadio Cuauhtémoc, Puebla.
Goals: Jan Ceulemans (11'), Jean-Marc Ferreri (27'), Jean-Pierre Papin (43'), Nico Claesen (73'), Bernard Genghini (104'), Manuel Amoros (pen) (111').

[Match] 1970 FIFA Men's World Cup · Peru vs Morocco · group stage · 1970-06-06
Score: 3-0. Stadium: Estadio Nou Camp, León.
Goals: Teófilo Cubillas (65'), Roberto Challe (67'), Teófilo Cubillas (75').
